---
title: "Pop Art and Duotone"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Pop Art and Duotone}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", eval = FALSE)
```

Pop art posterizes luminance into a few saturated colors. Duotone interpolates
tones between two chosen colors.

```{r}
library(ImageArtR)
img <- read_art_image(system.file("extdata", "example.png", package = "ImageArtR"))

list_pop_art_palettes()
pop <- image_pop_art(img, panels = 4, seed = 123)
duo <- image_duotone(img, shadow = "#172A3A", highlight = "#F4D35E", colorspace = "Lab")

plot(pop)
plot(duo)
```
